flashcards.definition: Systematic approach: identify → gather info → list alternatives → evaluate → decide → review.
Study of how psychological biases affect financial decisions (overconfidence, loss aversion, herd behavior).
The tendency to feel losses more strongly than equivalent gains — losing $100 hurts more than gaining $100 feels good.
Over-relying on the first piece of information encountered when making decisions (e.g., original price as anchor).
Tool for comparing options by scoring each against weighted criteria to identify the best choice.
